data.title: Toluene prices gain in the United States
data.description: On Wednesday, toluene prices journeyed northward in the US.An industry source in the US on condition of anonymity informed a Polymerupdate team member, "Toluene prices rose amid rising bids. Tight supplies further contributed to the pricing uptrend. Toluene prices were assessed at the 269.00-269.10 cents/gallon FOB US Gulf mark, higher by (+5 cents/gallon) from Tuesday's assessed levels.Meanwhile, downstream benzene prices on Wednesday were assessed at the 225.00-225.10 cents/gallon levels, a day on day fall of (-6 cents/gallon).

data.title: Fresh High density polyethylene (HDPE Injection) offers in Angola
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in Central Africa reported that a producer from Saudi Arabia is heard to have offered its HDPE Injection (M80064) grade at the USD 1220/mt levels.This offer is for April 2025 shipment on CFR Luanda port basis.

data.title: A producer from Saudi Arabia offers High density polyethylene (HDPE Injection) in Angola
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in Central Africa informed that a Saudi Arabian producer is heard to have announced its offer for HDPE Injection (M80064S UV Stabilized) grade at the USD 1280/mt levels.This offer is for April 2025 shipment on CFR Luanda port basis.

data.title: Market Chatter: Stellantis Halts Production of Leapmotor's T03 Model in Poland
data.description:
Stellantis has halted production of Zhejiang Leapmotor Technology's T03 small electric car in Poland, Reuters reported Tuesday.

The French-Italian automobile manufacturer stopped the assembly of the T03 model on March 30 and is currently gauging other production options for the vehicle, the report said.

While it did not specify the reason for the stoppage, Stellantis said it remains committed to launching Leapmotor vehicles in Europe, Reuters reported.

Stellantis and Leapmotor are now looking to upgrade the model to A-series models that will be produced outside China from 2026, Reuters reported, citing a person with knowledge of the matter.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Curevac Pre-Tax Loss Was EUR 38.6 Million For Fourth Quarter of 2024
data.description:
CUREVAC NV :

  • ANNOUNCES FINANCIAL RESULTS FOR THE FOURTH QUARTER AND FULL-YEAR 2024 AND PROVIDES BUSINESS UPDATE

  • COMPLETED ENROLLMENT OF PART B PHASE 1 GLIOBLASTOMA STUDY

  • CASH AND CASH EQUIVALENTS AMOUNTED TO EUR 481.7 MILLION AT END OF DECEMBER 2024, INCREASING FROM EUR 402.5 MILLION AT END OF 2023

  • REVENUES AMOUNTED TO EUR 14.5 MILLION AND EUR 535.2 MILLION FOR THREE AND TWELVE MONTHS ENDED DECEMBER 31, 2024, RESPECTIVELY, REPRESENTING A DECREASE OF EUR 8.1 MILLION AND AN INCREASE OF EUR 481.4 MILLION FROM EUR 22.6 MILLION AND EUR 53.8 MILLION FOR SAME PERIODS IN 2023.

  • IN 2024, CASH USED IN OPERATIONS WAS MAINLY ALLOCATED TO EXTRAORDINARY PAYMENTS AMOUNTING TO A TOTAL OF EUR 137 MILLION

  • COMPANY REAFFIRMS ITS EXPECTED CASH RUNWAY INTO 2028

  • OPERATING LOSS AMOUNTED TO EUR 43.8 MILLION FOR THREE MONTHS ENDED DECEMBER 31, 2024

  • REVENUE INCREASE YEAR-ON-YEAR WAS PRIMARILY DRIVEN BY NEW LICENSE AGREEMENT WITH GSK, WHICH CLOSED IN JULY 2024

  • JURY TRIAL IN U.S. LITIGATION POSTPONED TO SEPTEMBER 8, 2025

  • OPERATING PROFIT AMOUNTED TO EUR 177.7 MILLION FOR TWELVE MONTHS ENDED DECEMBER 31, 2024

  • PRE-TAX LOSS WAS EUR 38.6 MILLION FOR THREE MONTHS ENDED DECEMBER 31, 2024

  • PRE-TAX PROFIT WAS EUR 190.9 MILLION FOR TWELVE MONTHS ENDED DECEMBER 31, 2024

  • PART B OF GLIOSBLASTOMA STUDY FIRST DATA READOUT ANTICIPATED IN H2 2025

data.title: News Highlights: Top Financial Services News of the Day - Thursday at 7 AM ET
data.description:
Why the Trade War Isn't Over for America's Biggest Banks

As bank-earnings season begins Friday with JPMorgan, investors care more about the outlook than prior results.

The Dollar and the Bond Market's Ominous Message for Trump

In the turmoil touched off by a trade war, the U.S. is no longer seen as safe.

Accounting Firms Baker Tilly and Moss Adams Near Merger

Moss Adams would become partly owned by private equity through the deal, which would create a combined firm with more than $3 billion in annual revenue.

Investors in GPB Capital Funds May See Some Cash at Last

Thousands of investors in GPB Capital Holdings funds may soon start to get some of their money back after a federal judge authorized the firm's court-appointed receiver to begin an initial $400 million distribution.

Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on Barclays, Magellan and more in the latest Market Talks covering Financial Services.

Treasury Secretary Bessent expects bond market will calm down, suggests spike in yields is normal trading

"I believe there is nothing systemic about this," Bessent said.

Bond Rout Stirs Broader Fears About Market Strains

Treasurys, normally a haven, have sold off sharply this week.

ECB Has Many Instruments It Can Deploy, Cipollone Says

The European Central Bank has many instruments it can deploy in the event that economic disruption and sharp falls in financial markets threaten to turn into a crisis, a senior official said Wednesday.

Bain Capital to Take 9.9% Stake in Lincoln National

The two parties are set to enter into a 10-year, nonexclusive strategic investment-management relationship.

Why the Selloff in Treasurys Is Rattling Investors

A sharp rise in Treasury yields over the past few days has alarmed many analysts and investors much more than the recent drop in stocks.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Energy News of the Day - Thursday at 7 AM ET
data.description:
Equinor Creates New Power Business to Cater to Data-Center Demand

The company said growing demand for power as well as the expansion of AI and data centers drove the move.

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on PTT Exploration and Production, Santos and more in the latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities.

Oil Bounces as Trump Holds Off Some Tariffs for 90 Days

Crude futures climbed after President Trump paused some tariffs for 90 days, while raising tariffs on China further following Beijing's retaliation.

U.S. Crude Oil Inventories Rise for Second Straight Week

U.S. crude oil inventories rose for a second consecutive week, climbing 2.6 million barrels, amid an increase in net imports.

Trump Throws Money at Coal. Miner Stocks Love It.

The president signed an executive order designating coal as a "critical mineral."

Peabody Reviews Anglo American Coal Deal After Mine Fire

Peabody Energy said it is reviewing an up to $3.78 billion deal to buy Anglo American's steelmaking coal operations following a fire at an Australian mine.

The Stock Market Is Bad. The Oil Market Might Be Worse.

Oil prices are down 15% for the year, including a 2.1% fall on Monday.

Shell Lowers Gas Production Guidance

The energy giant lowered its integrated gas production estimate for the first quarter as volumes were hit by unplanned maintenance.

Trump Promised to Lower Energy Prices-but It Wasn't Supposed to Be Like This

The president's tariff blitz sent oil prices tumbling 14% in two days, bludgeoning frackers.

Woodside to Sell 40% Stake in Louisiana LNG Infrastructure

Stonepeak has agreed to invest $5.7 billion in Woodside Energy's proposed Louisiana LNG gas-export infrastructure in the U.S.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Thursday at 7 AM ET
data.description:
BMW Deliveries Slip on China Weakness, Despite Strength in Europe and U.S.

The automaker's deliveries fell 1.4% on year in the first quarter as strong growth in Europe and the U.S. failed to fully offset a demand slump in China.

Tesla Stock Soared 23%. There Are Still a Couple of Problems.

The EV maker's shares, along with those of other auto makers, rallied after President Donald Trump's decision to pause some tariffs. The car industry still faces stiff levies, though.

Volkswagen Earnings Miss Hopes on Higher Costs, Including Tariff Hit

First-quarter earnings missed expectations after taking a hit from one-off items including some early impact from U.S. import tariffs.

Uniqlo Owner Boosts Earnings Forecast

Fast Retailing raised its annual earnings forecast despite some impact from U.S. tariffs, after reporting higher first-half profit thanks partly to revenue growth across various regions.

Chicago Hot Dog Eatery Portillo's Slims Down Menus to Expand Nationally

Portillo's, the restaurant chain known for its beefy sandwiches and topping-heavy hot dogs, is slimming down to get bigger.

Inside Walmart's Meeting With Investors on a Day of Tariff Turmoil

As global markets gyrated over tariffs, Walmart executives gathered with their biggest investors in Dallas this week and worked to project calm.

Costco's March Was Strong. Tariff Uncertainty Didn't Stop Consumers.

Sales rose by 8.6% year over year in March.

Sweepstakes Company Publishers Clearing House Files for Bankruptcy

The direct-mail company's Prize Patrol gained fame delivering oversize checks with TV cameras running.

EQT Stake Sale Values Swedish Software Developer IFS at Over $16 Billion

The roughly $3.29 billion deal gives Hg control of the company alongside EQT.

Accounting Firms Baker Tilly and Moss Adams Near Merger

Moss Adams would become partly owned by private equity through the deal, which would create a combined firm with more than $3 billion in annual revenue.

data.title: Draganfly and SafeLane Global Enter into Multi-Year Agreement with Draganfly as the Preferred Global Provider of Landmine Mapping Drones and Aerial Survey Services
data.description:
First Ukraine Landmine Aerial Survey Contract Underway

Tampa, FL, April 10, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Draganfly Inc. (FSE: 3U8) (“Draganfly” or the “Company”), an industry-leading developer of drone solutions and systems, today announced that it has been selected by SafeLane Global Ltd. (“SafeLane”) as its preferred unmanned aerial systems (UAS) and aerial survey provider.

SafeLane, a world-renowned specialist in explosive threat mitigation, is one of only two private organizations licensed by the Ukrainian Ministry of Defense to conduct landmine and explosive ordnance clearance operations in Ukraine. With over 30 years of experience across more than 60 countries, SafeLane supports governments, humanitarian organizations, and commercial clients in the clearance and disposal of landmines, unexploded ordnance (UXO), and explosive remnants of war (ERW), both on land and underwater.

Under the agreement, Draganfly will provide advanced drone solutions, including UAVs, specialized sensors, and data analysis services, to support SafeLane’s global mine action initiatives. The collaboration aims to enhance the speed, accuracy, and safety of explosive threat detection and removal operations in high-risk environments.

“We are honored to be selected as SafeLane’s UAS partner,” said Cameron Chell, President and CEO of Draganfly. “This partnership represents a significant opportunity to leverage Draganfly’s technology to support critical humanitarian and defense efforts. Together, we will work to deliver scalable, innovative solutions for global landmine action.”

The companies will co-develop joint intellectual property and standard operating procedures tailored for aerial mine detection and clearance. SafeLane will lead proposal submissions and operational deployment, while Draganfly will provide technology, mission planning, piloting, and survey analysis.

According to the Landmine Monitor 2023, more than 60 million landmines remain buried across over 60 countries, posing a persistent threat to civilians, especially children, who account for nearly half of the casualties. Ukraine is currently one of the most mine-contaminated countries in the world.

“Draganfly’s drone-based technology will significantly increase the safety and efficiency of our operations,” said Asa Gilbert, Director of Business Development at SafeLane. “This partnership is a critical step in helping communities recover from the legacy of conflict.”

The collaboration further positions Draganfly as a key player in the defense and humanitarian sectors, supporting efforts to create safer environments in some of the world’s most vulnerable regions.

About Draganfly

Draganfly Inc. (NASDAQ: DPRO; CSE: DPRO; FSE: 3U8A) is a pioneer in drone solutions, AI-driven software, and robotics. With over 25 years of innovation, Draganfly has been at the forefront of drone technology, providing solutions for public safety, agriculture, industrial inspections, security, mapping, and surveying. The Company is committed to delivering efficient, reliable, and industry-leading technology that helps organizations save time, money, and lives.

For more information, visit www.draganfly.com

For investor details, visit:

CSE

NASDAQ

FRANKFURT

Media Contact

media@draganfly.com

Company Contact

info@draganfly.com

data.title: Carmax 4Q Profit Rises, Used-Car Prices Creep Up
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Carmax fourth-quarter profit rose as used-car prices inched up.

The used-car retailer posted a profit of $89.9 million, or 58 cents a share, in the three months ended in February, compared with $50.3 million, or 32 cents a share, a year earlier.

Revenue rose 7% to $6.0 billion, in line with what analysts polled by FactSet forecasted.

Same-store used-unit sales increased 5%. Total used-unit sales rose 6.2% and used vehicles' average selling price was 0.6% higher than a year before.

Selling, general and administrative expenses grew 5% primarily because of an increase in compensation and advertising spending, Carmax said.

If implemented, tariffs could be a mixed bag for Carmax, analysts say. Both new and used vehicle prices are set to rise due to increased costs for automakers. That could motivate some car shoppers to shift from new to used vehicles, but lower-income consumers may steer away from buying at all and try to repair and hang onto existing cars for longer.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Desjardins Says U.S. Treasuries Likely Behind Trump's 90-Day Surprise Tariffs Reprieve
data.description:
United States President Donald Trump's decision to offer a surprise 90-day reprieve to countries that didn't retaliate had stocks soaring into the close on Wednesday.

However, the prior declines in equity markets don't appear to be the catalyst for the president's sudden change of heart, said Desjardins. Rather, the brewing turmoil in the market for U.S. Treasuries seems to have been what drove the reversal.

In the days leading up to Wednesday's pause, interest rates on U.S. government debt had risen across the curve, with the long-end leading the way higher and exhibiting unusually high volatility, noted the bank. Late Wednesday, the 10-year bond yield broke above 4.50% and the 30-year yield revisited cycle highs around 5.00%.

Market participants were searching for answers about what was driving longer-term interest rates dramatically higher, stated Desjardins. A host of explanations were circulating, including China selling USTs, the unwinding of basis trades, elevated concerns about inflation and even USTs losing their safe haven status. Whatever caused the market moves, liquidity was thin and there were risks that market functioning could begin to deteriorate more meaningfully, which could have proven "catastrophic."

Both Trump and his advisors realized that pushing any harder on tariffs risked causing critical damage to the U.S. and global financial systems. The bond market once again played its role in regulating the government, according to the bank.

That said, while tariffs on most U.S. trading partners were dialed down Wednesday, levies on Chinese imports were raised to a "whopping" 125%. President Trump did offer a glimmer of hope for U.S.-China trade relations. While simultaneously praising President Xi Jinping, Trump said that he can't imagine tariffs rising to higher-than-current levels.

Whether or not those words set the stage for a less hawkish approach to China remains an open question, added Desjardins.

For its part, Canada is still stuck with the previously announced tariffs on steel, aluminum and the non-U.S. content of autos sent to its southern neighbor. As a result, whatever competitive advantage Canada could have gained from the application of tariffs on other countries has now been eroded.

However, Canada's economy is heavily dependent on the prospects for the U.S. and global economies, both of which have been at least temporarily upgraded following Trump's decision to pause the tariffs. In addition, it remains to be seen how much deflation Canada will now import from China as the latter tries to find markets other than the U.S. to sell more goods.

The situation remains fluid enough to push monetary policymakers at the Bank of Canada to the sidelines next week, according to Desjardins. However, as hard data begin to show the economic drag from the back-and-forth in U.S. trade policy, the bank expects the BoC to act decisively to support the economy with monetary easing.

data.title: Liquidia Price Target Maintained With a $25.00/Share by Needham
Serbia to sell 23% of bus company Lasta Lazarevac
data.description:
BELGRADE (Serbia), April 10 (SeeNews) - Serbia's economy ministry said it has launched a public auction for the sale of a 22.84% stake in bus company Lasta Lazarevac, at a starting price of 1.14 million euro ($1.26 million).

Eligible bidders are foreign and domestic legal entities as well as consortiums, the ministry said in a public call for offers last week.

The deadline for submission of bids is April 23.

The ministry said the starting price corresponds to the estimated asset value of the stake in Lasta Lazarevac for sale. The remainder of Lasta Lazarevac is owned by local transport company Strela-Obrenovac.

Headquartered in the Belgrade municipality of Lazarevac, Lasta Lazarevac engages in both domestic and international passenger transport. The company has a fleet of over 100 buses and over 300 employees, it says on its website.

In 2024, Lasta Lazarevac posted an operating revenue of 1.2 billion dinars ($11.3 million/ 10.2 million euro) and a net profit of 59 million dinars, according to its annual financial statement.

($ = 0.905 euro)

data.title: US fintechs fall as escalating US-China trade war extends market rout
data.description:
** Shares of U.S. fintech companies fall premarket, paring Wednesday's gains after U.S. President Donald Trump's move to temporarily lower the heavy tariffs on dozens of countries

** Trump also increased tariffs on China to 125% from 104%

** Affirm down 3.6%, Pagaya Technologies falls 3.3% and Upstart Holdings down 2.6%

** PayPal drops 1.9% and Block slips 1.7%

** LendingClub sheds 4% in choppy trading

** Despite the temporary reprieve on Wednesday, a trade war between the two largest economies in the world fuels uncertainty that could dampen consumer confidence and curb spending

** The 10% blanket duty on almost all U.S. imports and separate duties on autos, steel and aluminum will remain in effect

** Swedish fintech Klarna paused its plans for a U.S. IPO last Friday as investor and consumer sentiments eroded amid a tariff-induced global market rout

data.title: Euro Soars on Relief from Tariff Pause
data.description:
The euro continued its rally, approaching the $1.11 mark and reaching its highest level since late September 2024, after the European Union announced a 90-day suspension of new tariffs on the U.S. to allow for trade negotiations.

This move followed President Trump’s decision to reduce tariffs on countries that hadn’t retaliated against his initial trade levies, cutting them to 10%, while duties on Chinese imports were raised to 125% from 104%.

While the announcement helped ease concerns over a global economic slowdown and rising inflation, it also introduced new uncertainty that could persist for months.

In response, money markets adjusted their expectations for European Central Bank rate cuts, pricing in an ECB deposit facility rate of 1.8% by December, up from 1.65% on Wednesday and compared to 1.9% the previous week.

The probability of an April rate cut also dropped to 90%, down from full pricing just a day earlier.

data.title: CarMax Stock Tumbles on Earnings Miss
data.description:
By Mackenzie Tatananni

Shares of CarMax tumbled in premarket trading Thursday after the used-car dealer reported fiscal-fourth-quarter earnings that missed expectations.

For the quarter ended Feb. 28, CarMax reported earnings of 58 cents a share, below the 66 cents analysts anticipated, according to FactSet. Net revenue increased 6.7% from a year ago to $6 billion, roughly in line with Wall Street forecasts. The company noted that retail used unit sales rose 6.2% in the quarter, while comparable-store used unit sales increased 5.1% and wholesale units rose 3.1%.

Shares were down 7.1% to $74.44 in premarket trading. The stock closed up 9.2% on Wednesday.

Write to Mackenzie Tatananni at mackenzie.tatananni@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Verbund Romania acquires 272 MW wind project from Monsson
data.description:
BUCHAREST (Romania), April 10 (SeeNews) - Verbund Wind Power Romania, the local unit of Austrian energy company Verbund, has acquired a 272 MW wind park project from Romanian renewables developer Monsson, the two companies said.

The project, located in the western county of Caras-Severin, was acquired at the ready-to-build stage, with all relevant permits and connection points, and its construction is expected to begin in 2026, the two companies said in a joint press release on Wednesday. Once completed, the wind park is expected to generate around 569 GWh of green energy per year.

The value of the transaction was not disclosed.

Founded in 1997, Monsson has reached a project pipeline of over 7 GW of wind and solar capacity. Last month, Monsson said it will build a factory for the production and assembly of components for the renewables industry near the former Petrila coal mine, in the western county of Hunedoara.

Verbund has been active in Romania since 2012, operating the 226 MW Casimcea wind farm in the southeastern county of Tulcea. It also has a portfolio of wind and photovoltaic projects in development in the country.

data.title: Equinor Combines Renewables, Flexible Power Under New Business
data.description:
Equinor said Thursday that it is establishing a new power business by combining its renewables portfolio with flexible power assets from its marketing, midstream, and processing business.

The company said the new business is intended to cater to growing power demand as electrification continues, along with the expansion of data centers and artificial intelligence.

Equinor said gas and power trading and market analysis group will remain under its marketing, midstream, and processing business.
